data.title: AMMB Holdings Gains a Bull on Improving Outlook — Market Talk
data.description: AMMB Holdings gains a bull in CIMB Securities after strong 2Q results prompt the brokerage to raise its FY 2026 earnings forecast by 8.3%. Analyst Ei Leen Tan says in a note that the earnings upgrade factor in robust noninterest income growth, lower net provisions/impairments and more resilient net interest margin. Tan also lifts the FY 2027 earnings forecast by 3.1%. The revisions are despite factoring in a potential additional 25bp overnight policy rate cut in 2Q 2026, Tan says. CIMB upgrades AMMB to buy from hold and raises the target price to MYR6.70 from MYR5.80. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

data.title: Key facts: Franklin Templeton, Grayscale launch XRP ETFs; $33.6B inflows possible; XRP price drop triggers $1.14M liquidations
data.description:
• Franklin Templeton's XRPZ ETF debuted on NYSE Arca, showcasing XRP's role in cross-currency settlements. Grayscale also launched its GXRP ETF, both achieving record performances.1
  • Analysis shows that $33.6 billion in annual ETF inflows could allow institutions to buy most of XRP's supply within a year, potentially depleting it in under six months.2
  • XRP's price drop led to $1.14 million in liquidations in 12 hours, with a 1,447% imbalance. Long positions made up $1.23 million, while shorts lost $85,580.3

data.title: Key facts: Bitcoin drops 25% in November; Texas invests $5M in Bitcoin Trust; MSTR faces MSCI review
data.description:
• In November, Bitcoin (BTCUSD) fell nearly 25%, dropping from over $126,000 to about $81,000, before recovering to around $87,000, impacting the crypto market by over $1 trillion.1
  • Texas bought $5 million in BlackRock iShares Bitcoin Trust on November 20, 2025, as part of a $10 million investment in its Strategic Bitcoin Reserve, marking a historic first.23
  • Concerns grow over MSTR, a major Bitcoin holder, as MSCI reviews index criteria. Exclusion could trigger institutional selling, impacting Bitcoin's price ahead of a decision by January 15, 2026.4

data.title: Key facts: US Bank tests stablecoin on Stellar; pilot program for banks
data.description:
• US Bank is testing stablecoin issuance on the Stellar network, boosting Stellar's status as a trusted platform for financial institutions and encouraging more institutional adoption.1
  • The Stellar network is part of a pilot program demonstrating blockchain's benefits for banks, focusing on transaction unwinding and asset freezing for enhanced customer protection.2

data.title: Key facts: S&P Downgrades Tether's USDT; Tether Holds 116 Tons of Gold
data.description:
• S&P Global Ratings downgraded Tether's USDT to '5 (weak)' due to worries about its dollar peg, citing higher-risk assets in reserves and ongoing disclosure gaps.12
  • Tether possesses 116 tons of physical gold, making it one of the largest gold holders outside central banks, with reserves similar to South Korea and Hungary.3

data.title: Key facts: Grayscale files for Zcash Trust spot ETF; regulatory issues noted
data.description:
• Grayscale has filed to convert its Zcash Trust into a spot ETF, potentially the first for a privacy coin. The Trust manages over $196 million in assets.12
  • Grayscale's filing points out regulatory issues faced by Zcash, including delistings in late 2023 and early 2024 due to increased scrutiny on privacy coins.3

data.title: Key facts: Ethereum dips below $3,000; Buterin forecasts growth; Fusaka upgrade enhances Layer 2
data.description:
• Ethereum's price fell below $3,000, reaching a demand zone around $2,680 on November 21, followed by a 10% rebound to $2,970, with the RSI trendline being reclaimed.1
  • Vitalik Buterin predicts Ethereum will grow next year, potentially raising the gas limit. However, some operations may cost more to improve network security as it scales.2
  • The Fusaka upgrade for Ethereum will introduce Peer Data Availability Sampling (PeerDAS), allowing EVM nodes to verify data availability efficiently, enhancing Layer 2 operations.3

data.title: Key facts: Crypto Copilot steals Solana; price rises above $133; DWF Labs launches $75M fund
data.description:
• A report reveals that the 'Crypto Copilot' Chrome extension is stealing Solana (SOL) from users by adding hidden instructions to transactions, diverting funds to the attacker's wallet.1
  • Solana's price has climbed above the 100-hour moving average, backed by a bullish trend line at $133, with potential gains if it breaks resistance at $140 and $142.2
  • DWF Labs has launched a $75 million fund for DeFi projects, focusing on Solana to tackle liquidity, settlement, credit, and on-chain risk management challenges.3

data.title: Key facts: Cardano shows price stabilization; 1,683 holders face losses
data.description:
• Cardano (ADAUSD) shows potential price stabilization with a falling-wedge pattern, a bullish signal. The RSI is at 30, indicating oversold conditions, while positive funding hints at a price rise.1
  • Over seven million ADA held by 1,683 holders face loss due to delegation to an inactive stake pool. Users should switch to active pools to earn staking rewards.2

data.title: Australia's gold stocks rise on firm bullion prices
data.description:
** Australian gold stocks rise as much as 2.8% to hit their highest level since October 20

** Gold prices hovered near a more than one-week high on Wednesday, buoyed by expectations of a U.S. interest rate cut next month

** Sector heavyweights Northern Star Resources and Evolution Mining advance 2.7% and 2.2%, respectively

** Sub-index has more than doubled in value so far this year

data.title: Studds Accessories Q2 Results: Revenue, margin expands; Announces deal with Decathlon
data.description:
Faridabad, Haryana-based motorcycle accessories and helmet manufacturer Studds Accessories Ltd. announced its September quarter results after market hours on Wednesday, November 26. The stock will react to these numbers in today's trading session. These were the first numbers declared by the company after its listing earlier this month.

Revenue for the company grew by 6.5% from last year to ₹154.4 crore from ₹145 crore earlier. Its Earnings Before Interest, Tax, Depreciation and Amortisation (EBITDA) grew by 12% on a year-on-year basis to ₹29.9 crore from ₹26.7 crore earlier.

EBITDA margin expanded by 90 basis points to 19.3% from 18.4% last year, while its net profit grew by 17.9% to ₹20.6 crore from ₹17.5 crore during the year-ago period.

For the first half of the financial year, Studds Accessories' revenue grew 6.4%, EBITDA was up 17.9%, while margins expanded by nearly 200 basis points to 19.8%.

The company also announced that it has onboarded Decathlon as a new customer to serve its India requirements for bicycle helmets and allied products. Investments are currently being made in new moulds, with commercial production expected to commence by the end of the fourth quarter of financial year 2026.

To cater to increasing demand, the company will be expanding its capacity for two-wheeler helmets and boxes by 1.5 million units in the first phase, which will be operational by the first quarter of financial year 2027, according to its press release. "This expansion will enhance our ability to service both domestic and export markets while providing headroom for future growth initiatives," Managing Director Sidhartha Bhushan Khurana was quoted as saying.

Studds Accessories' board has also approved the setting up of a new warehouse in Spain to strengthen its distribution in Europe, improve service and support product portfolio expansion.

Shares of Studds Accessories ended 1.2% higher on Wednesday ahead of its results announcement. The stock is still trading below its IPO price of ₹585 per share, having listed at a discount to their issue price.

data.title: Brunei Trade Surplus Widens
data.description:
Brunei’s trade surplus increased to BND 354.3 million in September 2025 from BND 339.5 million in the same month a year earlier.

Year-on-year, exports dropped 12.1% to BND 1.03 billion, primarily due to lower shipments of mineral fuels (-10.5%), chemicals (-18.3%), and machinery and transport equipment (-24.1%).

Key export destinations included Australia (22.2% of total shipments), China (21.2%), Singapore (16.5%), Japan (9.6%), and Taiwan (8.4%).

Meanwhile, imports plunged 18.8% to BND 670.7 million, reflecting weaker demand for mineral fuels (-22.7%), machinery and transport equipment (-17.8%), and miscellaneous manufactured articles (-9.9%).

Malaysia remained the top import source (27.0%), followed by Singapore (18.8%), Australia (9.9%), the United Arab Emirates (7.4%), China (5.9%), and the U.S. (3.9%).

For the first nine months of the year, the trade surplus edged down to BND 3.98 billion from BND 4.04 billion a year earlier, as exports shrank 13.1% while imports tumbled 19.2%.

data.title: Harvey Norman Loses Bull as Housing Outlook Dims — Market Talk
data.description: Harvey Norman is downgraded to neutral from outperform by Macquarie, following its share price rally and a dimming Australian housing outlook. Macquarie says a meaningful improvement in new detached housing is key to momentum within Harvey Norman's Australian business. However, additional interest-rate cuts look unlikely as inflationary pressures reignite. Macquarie says some support for sales is coming from consumers replacing old technology with new products, but "the potential for further upside surprises as more limited." Harvey Norman's share price is up more than 40% over the past 12 months, and Macquarie says risk and reward for investors have become more balanced. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Rio Tinto's Coming Investor Briefing Seen Focused on Costs, Lithium, Asset Sales — Market Talk
data.description: Investors will seek a few things when Rio Tinto holds its capital markets day next week, Morgan Stanley analysts say. Firstly, "investors will look for quantifiable cost reduction targets--both opex and capex making it more competitive vs peers such as BHP," the analysts say in a note. In lithium, Rio Tinto is expected to trim its growth pipeline and phase projects more gradually. It could also closely assess the carrying value of its lithium book, the analysts say. The market will be eager to hear potential plans to exit noncore businesses such as borates and titanium. "In our view, a clear articulation of disposal priorities and timelines would boost investor confidence." MS has an equal-weight rating and A$129.50 target. The stock is little changed at A$134.20. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)
